patch.value: Declarative extension install: activation failure is misreported and scaffold next_steps break when @unbrained/pm-cli is unresolvable

Installing a declarative (composeExtension) package into a workspace that cannot resolve @unbrained/pm-cli fails runtime activation ('Cannot find package'), yet pm install reports activated:true with runtime_activation_status:failed and failed_count:0 (contradictory). The failed install-time activation also skips item-type folder scaffolding, leaving a lingering health warning. Scaffold next_steps do not warn that the workspace must be able to resolve the SDK. Found in 2026-07-02 audit (Lane B, pm-6vy7).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: 2026-07-04 sandbox audit adds evidence: same misreporting class on IMPERATIVE installs — pm extension --install reports ok:true while activation_status:failed; failure surfaces only as raw repeated Node stderr warnings (11 lines per subsequent command) and --list shows contradictory active:true/enabled:true/activation_status:failed. Install should run an activation probe and fail loudly. Root cause of the sandbox failures themselves is tracked separately (pm-r0m4 missing type:module).

patch.value: Declarative extension install reports activation failure accurately and scaffold next_steps work when @unbrained/pm-cli is unresolvable; regression test covers the unresolvable-host path.

## Context
Found in the 2026-07-02 ecosystem audit (Lane B, pm-6vy7). Declarative (composeExtension) package installs into a workspace that cannot resolve @unbrained/pm-cli fail runtime activation with 'Cannot find package', yet the install result claims success.

## Defects
1. Contradictory report: pm install prints activated:true with runtime_activation_status:failed and failed_count:0 — an agent reading any one field gets a different story.
2. The failed install-time activation skips item-type folder scaffolding, leaving a lingering pm health warning (overlaps pm-l98s, which covers the scaffolding gap generally).
3. Scaffold next_steps never warn that the target workspace must be able to resolve the SDK.

## Approach
Make activation status single-source: activated must be false (or partial) whenever runtime_activation_status is failed, and failed_count must count it. Add an install-time preflight that checks @unbrained/pm-cli resolvability from the workspace and emits an actionable hint (install dep / use bundled catalog). Extend scaffold next_steps with the resolvability requirement.

## Definition of done
Sandbox repro (CJS + no SDK dep) shows coherent failure reporting with a recovery hint; health warning does not linger after a successful retry; spec covers the contradictory-status regression.
